verses.text: Joel calls the people to tell their children of this disaster: wave after wave of locusts has stripped the land bare. A memorable plague. God's judgments are to be remembered and recounted.
Joel calls the people to tell their children of this disaster: wave after wave of locusts has stripped the land bare. A memorable plague. God's judgments are to be remembered and recounted.

Awake, ye drunkards, for 'a nation... strong, and without number' has laid the vine waste. Pleasure interrupted. The careless are roused by a calamity they cannot ignore.
Awake, ye drunkards, for 'a nation... strong, and without number' has laid the vine waste. Pleasure interrupted. The careless are roused by a calamity they cannot ignore.
Awake, ye drunkards, for 'a nation... strong, and without number' has laid the vine waste. Pleasure interrupted. The careless are roused by a calamity they cannot ignore.
Mourn like a bride for the husband of her youth; the offerings are cut off, and the priests grieve. Worship halted. When the land fails, even the service of God is disrupted.

Mourn like a bride for the husband of her youth; the offerings are cut off, and the priests grieve. Worship halted. When the land fails, even the service of God is disrupted.
Be ashamed, ye farmers; the harvest is perished, the trees withered, 'joy is withered away from the sons of men.' Gladness gone. Sin's harvest is barrenness and sorrow.
Be ashamed, ye farmers; the harvest is perished, the trees withered, 'joy is withered away from the sons of men.' Gladness gone. Sin's harvest is barrenness and sorrow.
Gird and fast, ye priests; sanctify an assembly, for 'the day of the LORD is at hand.' Call to repent. The plague is a herald of a greater day of reckoning.
Gird and fast, ye priests; sanctify an assembly, for 'the day of the LORD is at hand.' Call to repent. The plague is a herald of a greater day of reckoning.
Gird and fast, ye priests; sanctify an assembly, for 'the day of the LORD is at hand.' Call to repent. The plague is a herald of a greater day of reckoning.
Is not the food cut off before our eyes? The seed rots, the beasts groan for want of pasture. Universal want. All creation suffers under the judgment for sin.

'O LORD, to thee will I cry,' for fire has devoured the pastures, and even the beasts cry to Him. A turning to God. Distress drives both man and beast to the Maker.
